content.full_text:
RAV1 MERTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
July 18,
1994
Betsy Hanna Dixon
Rutan
& Tucker,
LLP
Attorneys at
Law
611 Anton Boulevard,
Suite 1400
Costa Mesa,
CA
92626-1998
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96-203
Dear Ms.
Dixon:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Board of Supervisors of Riverside County regarding
Government Code Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) 1
Please note that nothing in this letter should be
construed to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken
place.
QUESTIONS
1)
Does Section 84308 apply to the Riverside Board of
Supervisors that also serves in its entirety as the governing
board of the Riverside County Sanitation District
(“District”)
pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section 4730.8.?
2)
If Section 84308 does apply and the majority of the board
must disqualify themselves from a proceeding before the District,
would the rule of legally required participation permit them to
participate?
CONCLUSION
1)
Section 84308 does not apply to the Board of Supervisors
that sits in its entirety as the governing board of the District
because Section 84308 applies only to appointed officers.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Commission regulations
appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections 18000-18954.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Our File No. A-96-203
Page
2
2)
The rule of legally required participation applies to
appointed officials disqualified from a proceeding under Section
84308 in some circumstances.
ANALYSIS
Section 84308
In 1983,
Section 84308 was added to the Act in order to
ensure that appointed members of boards or commissions would not
be biased by contributors or potential contributors of large
campaign contributions who might appear before them in a
proceeding involving a license,
permit or entitlement for use.
Section 84308 applies to all appointed officers of any state
or local government agency, with the exception of the courts or
any agency in the judicial branch of government,
the Legislature,
the Board of Equalization,
and constitutional officers. This would
include persons appointed to the governing board of special
districts
2
As you know,
Section 84308 imposes two requirements on
appointed officers subject to this statute.
First,
no officer may
accept, solicit,
or direct a contribution of more than $250,
from
any party,
or his or her agent,
or from any participant, or his or
her agent, while a proceeding involving a license, permit,
or
other entitlement for use is pending before the agency and for
three months following the date a final decision is rendered in
the proceeding.
(Section 84308 (b) .)
Second, if an officer has received a contribution of more
than $250 during the past
12 months from a party or participant in
a proceeding involving a license, permit or other entitlement for
use pending before an agency,
the officer must disclose that fact
on the record of the proceeding and must disqualify himself or
herself from participating.
(Section 84308 (c).)
You have asked whether Section 84308 applies to the governing
board of the Sanitation District which is composed solely of the
Board of Supervisors pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section
4730.8.
You indicate that under Health and Safety Code Section
4730,
the board could also be made up of city and county
representatives which would create a
“mixed” board.
However,
our
analysis will focus on how the board is actually organized.
(Markman Advice Letter,
No.
1-93-217.)
(Copy enclosed.)
2
Section 82048.5 defines special district as
“any agency of the
state established for the local performance of governmental or
proprietary functions within limited boundaries.”
(Emphasis
added.)


Our File No.
A-96-203
Page
3
Because Section 84308 applies only to appointed officials,
Section 84308 (a) (3)
excludes from the definition of “agency” local
governmental agencies whose members are directly elected by the
voters.
Regulation 18438.1(a)
interprets this exclusion to mean
that officers of an agency are exempt from Section 84308 (a) (3)
when:
They are acting as members of the governing body of
the agency,
and the body is acting in its entirety as
itself or as the ex officio governing body of any other
agency.
(emphasis added)
Thus,
elected officers of an agency are not considered to be
appointed when they act as members of a governing body,
and the
body is acting in its entirety
3 as the governing body of any other
agency.
Because all of the members of the Board of Supervisors of
Riverside County are acting as the governing board of the
District,
they are excluded from the coverage of Section 84308.
By sitting in its entirety as the governing board of the District,
the members of the Board of Supervisors are not considered to be
appointed officials.
When a person is “elected” to the Board of
Supervisors of Riverside County, that person is,
in effect,
“elected” to the governing board of the District.
Rule of Legally Reguired Participation
You also have asked whether the rule of “legally required
participation” would apply if the members of the governing board
of the District were subject to Section 84308.
Section 87101 and
Regulation 18701 provide a limited exception if the official’s
participation is legally required.
This exception has been
applied to disqualifications based on Section 84308 under some
circumstances.
(Gilbert Advice Letter,
No.
1-93-217.)
(Copy
enclosed.)
If there is a specific decision for which you seek
advice on whether the rule of legally required participation would
apply,
please feel free to seek further assistance.
If the governing board of the District was not composed solely
of members of the Board of Supervisors,
the members of the
governing body would be subject to Section 84308.
(Markman Advice
Letter,
supra.)


Our File No.
A-96-203
Page
4
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Julia Butcher
Graduate Student Assistant,
Legal Division
ENCLOSURES

appointed officials disqualified from a proceeding under Section
84308 in some circumstances.
